ddls:
CREATE TABLE "mcs" (
  "mcs_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each MCS */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the modulation and coding scheme */,
  "stream" INTEGER /* Number of spatial streams associated with this MCS */,
  "modulation_type" TEXT /* Type of modulation used */,
  "coding_rate" REAL /* Coding rate of the MCS */,
  "detailed_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the MCS */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mcs_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric record */,
  "mcs_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the modulation and coding scheme */,
  "ht20_gi_0" REAL /* Throughput performance with HT20 and GI=0 */,
  "ht40_gi_0" REAL /* Throughput performance with HT40 and GI=0 */,
  "ht20_gi_1" REAL /* Throughput performance with HT20 and GI=1 */,
  "ht80_gi_0" REAL /* Throughput performance with HT80 and GI=0 */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment where metrics were measured */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_mcs_id FOREIGN KEY ("mcs_id") REFERENCES mcs ("mcs_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "wireless_standards" (
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each wireless standard */,
  "standard_name" TEXT /* Name of the wireless standard (e.g., Wi-Fi, LTE) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the wireless standard */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the standard */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the standard */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration test */,
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the wireless standard */,
  "mcs_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the modulation and coding scheme */,
  "frequency_band" TEXT /* Frequency band used in this configuration */,
  "channel_bandwidth" TEXT /* Channel bandwidth used */,
  "transmit_power" TEXT /* Transmit power level used */,
  "antenna_configuration" TEXT /* Antenna configuration used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_standard_id FOREIGN KEY ("standard_id") REFERENCES wireless_standards ("standard_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_mcs_id FOREIGN KEY ("mcs_id") REFERENCES mcs ("mcs_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test result */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the configuration tested */,
  "result_date" TEXT /* Date when the test was conducted */,
  "result_value" REAL /* Result value from the performance test */,
  "distance" INTEGER /* Distance between transmitter and receiver during the test */,
  "obstructions" TEXT /* Description of obstructions during the test */,
  "environment_conditions" TEXT /* Environment conditions during the test (e.g., temperature, humidity) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, researcher, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access occurred */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action taken by the user (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Data included in the report */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "checklists" (
  "checklist_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each checklist item */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the related test result */,
  "item_description" TEXT /* Description of the checklist item */,
  "is_completed" INTEGER /* Status of the checklist item (completed or not) */,
  "due_date" TEXT /* Due date for the checklist item */,
  "completion_date" TEXT /* Date the checklist item was completed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("checklist_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_checklists_test_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_id") REFERENCES test_results ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user giving feedback */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* Date feedback was provided */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or suggestions from the user */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of feedback (e.g., usability, feature request) */,
  "follow_up_status" TEXT /* Status of follow-up on the feedback (e.g., pending, addressed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "visualizations" (
  "viz_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each visualization */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the visualization */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Data source used for the visualization */,
  "viz_type" TEXT /* Type of visualization (e.g., chart, graph) */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date the visualization was created */,
  "configuration" TEXT /* Configuration details of the visualization */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments on the visualization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("viz_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_visualizations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the setting was last updated */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the setting (e.g., user, system) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving notifications */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., performance alert) */,
  "is_enabled" INTEGER /* Status of the notification setting (enabled or disabled) */,
  "preference" TEXT /* User's preferred method of receiving notifications */,
  "custom_message" TEXT /* Custom message for the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
